# Project Status Report — Agent Environment Launcher

**Date:** 2026-03-29  
**Version:** 3.2.3  
**Generated by:** GitHub Copilot (automated)

---

## 1. Executive Summary

The Agent Environment Launcher project is in an advanced state of completion, with 208 of 212 workpackages (98.1%) finished and 54 of 55 user stories closed. The remaining open work is confined to Windows code signing (2 WPs under US-040). Bug closure rate stands at 94.2% with 6 bugs in "Fixed" status awaiting formal closure and 1 low-severity bug still open. The overall test pass rate is 95.4% across 2,113 recorded test results. The project is production-ready on all three platforms, with Windows code signing as the only outstanding feature gap.

## 3. Category Progress

### INS — Installer
`[███████████████████░] 92%` — 23 / 25 Done  
Two WPs remain for Windows Authenticode signing (INS-024, INS-025). All other installer work including PyInstaller config, Inno Setup, DMG builder, and AppImage is complete.

### SAF — Safety / Security Gate
`[████████████████████] 100%` — 52 / 52 Done  
All security gate workpackages complete, including zone enforcement, denial counter, session scoping, tool access controls, and the v3.2.1 feedback-driven hardening cycle (SAF-046 through SAF-052).

## 5. Open Items & Risks

### Open Workpackages (2)

| ID | Name | Description |
|----|------|-------------|
| INS-024 | Integrate Authenticode signing into CI/CD pipeline | Add Windows code signing via SignPath.io or similar service to the GitHub Actions release workflow |
| INS-025 | Verify signed installer passes SmartScreen | End-to-end validation that the signed .exe installs without SmartScreen warnings |

### Open Bugs (1)

| ID | Severity | Description |
|----|----------|-------------|
| BUG-102 | Low | `validate_file_search` only name-checks `.github`, `.vscode`, `noagentzone`. Relative `.git/` queries bypass validation |

### Fixed Bugs Awaiting Closure (6)

| ID | Severity | Description |
|----|----------|-------------|
| BUG-111 | Medium | AGENT-RULES allows workspace root reads for config files; security gate denied them |
| BUG-113 | Medium | Memory tool access not enabled in security gate per AGENT-RULES |
| BUG-114 | Low | AGENT-RULES labels grep_search/file_search as "no zone restriction" — inaccurate |
| BUG-115 | Medium | Unfiltered grep_search returns workspace root file matches that read_file denies |
| BUG-117 | Medium | Window title showed "Turbulence Solutions Launcher" instead of "TS - Safe Agent Environment" |
| BUG-119 | High | Reset Agent Blocks UI had workspace selector but no execute button |

### Identified Risks

1. **Windows Code Signing Cost:** Authenticode certificates require an annual subscription. The recommended approach (SignPath.io) needs organizational setup.
2. **macOS Distribution:** Ad-hoc signing requires users to manually remove quarantine flags. This creates friction for non-technical users.
3. **Linux Testing Gap:** No real user has tested the AppImage. Issues may exist that CI cannot detect (desktop integration, file associations, permission models).
4. **Fixed-Not-Closed Bug Backlog:** 6 bugs are functionally resolved but lack formal Closed status, indicating a process gap in the bug lifecycle.
5. **Test Failure Ratio:** 96 failed test results (4.6%) are recorded. While some may be historical/superseded, the ratio warrants review.

## 7. Outlook & Roadmap

### Short-term (next 1–2 weeks)
- **Windows Code Signing (INS-024, INS-025):** Integrate Authenticode signing into the CI/CD pipeline using SignPath.io. This is the last open user story (US-040) and will eliminate SmartScreen warnings for Windows users.
- **Bug Closure Sweep:** Close the 6 bugs in "Fixed" status (BUG-111, 113, 114, 115, 117, 119) after verifying their fixes are merged to main.
- **BUG-102 Resolution:** Decide whether to fix the `file_search` relative `.git/` query bypass or accept the low-severity risk.

### Mid-term (1–2 months)
- **Certification Pipeline Template:** Build the second project template (`templates/certification-pipeline/`) referenced in the project scope. The GUI already has a placeholder "Coming Soon" entry.
- **Linux User Validation:** Recruit a Linux tester or set up a VM-based test environment to validate the AppImage on Ubuntu 22.04+ and Fedora 38+.
- **Test Result Cleanup:** Archive or review the 96 failed test results to establish a clean baseline. Ensure all current tests pass.

### Long-term (3–6 months)
- **Apple Developer Signing:** Evaluate obtaining an Apple Developer certificate ($99/year) for proper macOS notarization, eliminating the quarantine workaround.
- **Auto-Update Enhancement:** Improve the update UX with download progress indicators, rollback on failure, and delta updates.
- **Template Ecosystem Expansion:** Enable user-contributed templates with a template registry and validation framework.

---

## 8. Feature Ideas & Innovation Proposals

### 8.1 — Workspace Health Dashboard
**Description:** Add a built-in dashboard to the launcher that shows real-time security gate statistics per workspace: denial count, blocked commands, zone violations, and session history. Provides visibility into how agents interact with the safety boundaries.  
**Effort:** Medium  
**Impact:** High  
**Category:** UX / Security

### 8.2 — Template Marketplace with Safety Ratings
**Description:** Create a template registry where project templates are published with safety ratings (based on security gate strictness, zone count, and audit results). Users can browse, compare, and install templates directly from the launcher.  
**Effort:** High  
**Impact:** High  
**Category:** Community / Tooling

### 8.3 — Agent Behavior Replay & Audit Trail
**Description:** Record all tool calls, file accesses, and terminal commands during an agent session into a structured audit log. Provide a replay viewer that shows exactly what an agent did, when, and what was blocked — enabling post-session security review.  
**Effort:** Medium  
**Impact:** High  
**Category:** Security / DevOps

### 8.4 — One-Click Environment Snapshot & Restore
**Description:** Allow users to snapshot an entire workspace state (files, settings, agent configuration) and restore it later. Useful for creating reproducible starting points for experiments or rolling back after an agent makes undesirable changes.  
**Effort:** Medium  
**Impact:** Medium  
**Category:** UX / Tooling

### 8.5 — Cross-Platform Portable Mode
**Description:** Add a "portable mode" that runs entirely from a USB drive or cloud-synced folder without installation. The launcher, templates, and Python environment are self-contained. Ideal for developers who work across multiple machines or restricted environments.  
**Effort:** High  
**Impact:** Medium  
**Category:** DevOps / UX

### 8.6 — Security Gate Policy Editor
**Description:** Provide a visual editor in the launcher settings for customizing security gate policies per template: allowed zones, terminal command allowlists, tool restrictions, and denial thresholds. Changes are validated against a schema before applying.  
**Effort:** Medium  
**Impact:** High  
**Category:** Security / UX

### 8.7 — Agent Performance Benchmarking
**Description:** Integrate a lightweight benchmarking framework that measures agent task completion time, tool call efficiency, and denial rate across sessions. Display trends over time to help users optimize their agent configurations and identify bottleneck patterns.  
**Effort:** Low  
**Impact:** Medium  
**Category:** Tooling

---

## 9. Conclusion

The Agent Environment Launcher has reached a mature state at version 3.2.3, with 98.1% of all workpackages complete and all three target platforms building successfully. The security gate has been significantly hardened through the v3.2.1 feedback cycle, and the remaining work is limited to Windows code signing — a distribution concern rather than a functional gap. The project is ready for production use with the documented platform-specific workarounds, and the roadmap focuses on polish, ecosystem expansion, and long-term sustainability.
